pmos /magazine Stage-B reconciliation + durable items JSON + feed-health — implementation plan (260624-9fw)
Created using pmos‑learnkit

/magazine Stage-B reconciliation + durable items JSON + feed-health — implementation plan (260624-9fw)

Overview

Story 260624-9fw (route: skill, epic 260624-2bm) hardens the Stage-B / render / feed-health cluster of /magazine (pmos-learnkit). It implements design decisions D3 (suggest-only feed quarantine), D4 (GUID reconciliation), D5 (durable per-issue items JSON), D6 (overlapping-window reuse), and D8 (sidecar-only library, no backfill). Spec = the epic design doc (../../02_design.html); cited by anchor throughout.

Dependency: 260624-xck (windowing + single state.snapshot + state-schema groundwork) is assumed present. This plan adds only 9fw's work and never re-plans xck.

Done when: bash tests/structure.test.sh exits 0 with 0 failures; the new in-file --selftest harnesses behaviorally prove reconciliation (safe-ified key matches), sidecar persistence (items JSON written with bullets), library-from-sidecars + loud skip-notice (sidecar-less issue named, omitted, no crash), takeaway reuse (cached items not re-summarized), and feed-health (3 failing runs cross the named threshold, a success resets); the dogfood run (T8) is judged SHIP on both objective and subjective criteria; the issue/library HTML contract (reference/issue-format.md) is byte-stable for unchanged inputs.

Code Study Notes

Patterns to follow

  • In-file --selftest harnesses guarded by if (require.main === module) with a hermetic fs.mkdtempSync root and an assert(cond, msg) helper — every script follows this (magazine-run.js:455+, render-issue.js:340+, magazine-state.js selftest).
  • tests/structure.test.sh uses chk "label" "shell-test-expr" — assertions are either a passing --selftest or a grep -q for a named function/marker (e.g. render-issue.js:95 grid-dedup check). New behavior is wired as one selftest + one grep marker each.
  • Feed identity is the slug, not the URL — cursors key by slug via remapCursors/readFeedsTyped (magazine-run.js:106-123, FR-R4). Feed-health must key by the same slug (Inv-2).
  • State mutation goes through magazine-state.js helpers (transition, save, discover); the schema is {cursors, items, links} and is absence-tolerant on read.

Existing code to reuse

  • scripts/magazine-run.js:42-44 — safeGuid(guid) = String(guid).replace(/[^A-Za-z0-9._-]/g,'_'). The one safe-ify rule; moved to a shared module in T1 (Inv-2 "one home").
  • scripts/render-issue.js — cardHtml (:200) reads it.bullets/it.guid/it.tags; renderIssue (:244); renderLibrary (:292-308) consumes {issues:[{date,file,items:[…]}]}; dedupeItems grid backstop (:95); CLI dispatch at :412-423 (issue/library both write to stdout).
  • scripts/magazine-run.js — fetchOne(feed, since, max) (:94-108) is the single feed-fetch chokepoint; its catch at :104 is where a feed failure is observed (returns [] + one stderr line today). The natural hook for feed-health recording.
  • scripts/magazine-state.js — selftest + helper pattern; the home for feedHealth schema + recordFeedResult/feedsToSuggest.

Constraints discovered

  • render-issue.js issue writes HTML to stdout only — it has no output path, so the sidecar (D5) needs a new optional output-path argument; absent it, stdout behavior is preserved (back-compat for any caller).
  • SKILL.md Phase 5 (:198-199) hard-codes the two render commands — both lines must be rewritten when the CLI shape changes.
  • render-issue.js does no summarizer merge — the safe-ify drop (#3) happens in the Phase-4 agent layer. The code fix is a selftested reconciliation helper (T1) plus the Phase-4 prompt instruction (T2); D4 is belt-and-suspenders (verbatim echo prevents it, normalized fallback recovers it).
  • Trust rule (Inv-1) is load-bearing: an unmatched reconciliation key and a sidecar-less issue must both be surfaced loudly, never silently dropped.
  • Pure zero-dep Node ≥18; no package.json, no npm (Inv-3).

Stack signals

  • No host manifest in the skill dir — this is a bundled zero-dep Node skill. Reference system: the sibling /magazine scripts themselves (selftest + structure.test.sh conventions). Tests are bash + in-file node … --selftest.

Decision Log

#DecisionOptions consideredRationale
DL-1Shared GUID home = new scripts/lib-guid.js exporting safeGuid + normalizeGuid + matchByGuid; magazine-run.js and render-issue.js both require it.(a) new tiny module; (b) export from magazine-run.js; (c) put it in magazine-state.js.(b) drags the CLI's side-effect surface into render-issue.js; (c) overloads the state module with a string util. A dedicated 1-purpose module satisfies Inv-2 "one home" cleanly (FR-3.1, D4).
DL-2Sidecar trigger = optional 2nd positional <out-html> on issue mode; when present, write HTML there + sidecar <dir>/<date>_items.json (date parsed from the out filename). Absent → unchanged stdout.(a) positional out-path; (b) --items-out <path> flag; (c) a separate sidecar command.(a) is deterministic, needs no extra agent step (D5), and preserves the stdout contract for back-compat. (c) adds a redundant pass over the same data.
DL-3library accepts a directory → globs *_items.json sidecars; a sidecar-less *_issue.html is named in a loud stderr notice and omitted. The legacy library <issues.json> file-arg path is retained.(a) dir-glob + retain file arg; (b) hard switch to dir-only.D8 removes HTML parsing, not the JSON-file convenience; retaining the file arg avoids breaking any direct caller while the glob path becomes the documented default. No legacy HTML/pre-data-guid parser is introduced (D8).
DL-4Takeaway reuse = a deterministic mergeCachedBullets(snapshotItems, sidecarItems) helper + a Phase-5 doc step, not a pure agent instruction.(a) code helper + doc; (b) doc-only agent instruction.(b) is not selftestable and risks silent re-summarize/drop. A deterministic helper lets D6/AC5 be proven with a mixed cached/new fixture and keeps the trust rule enforceable.
DL-5Feed-health keyed by feed slug; threshold a named constant FEED_QUARANTINE_THRESHOLD = 3; suggestion is one compact stderr line per run; never auto-disable.(a) slug key; (b) URL key.(b) diverges from cursor keying (FR-R4) and would orphan on feed rename. Slug keying reuses the existing identity (Inv-2). Suggest-only honors Inv-1 (D3).

Tasks

T1: Shared GUID module + normalized reconciliation helper (tracer bullet)

Spec refs: FR-3.1, D4, Inv-2 (../../02_design.html#decisions, #invariants). Slice shape: vertical — smallest end-to-end of the GUID-drop fix (the riskiest unproven point: cross-keyed match).

Depends on: none.

  1. Create scripts/lib-guid.js exporting three pure functions:
    • safeGuid(guid) — moved verbatim from magazine-run.js:42-44.
    • normalizeGuid(guid) — returns safeGuid(String(guid)) (the single normalization used for matching).
    • matchByGuid(manifestGuids, key) — returns the manifest GUID whose normalizeGuid equals normalizeGuid(key) (exact-first, then normalized), else null (never a silent pick).
  2. Add an in-file --selftest to lib-guid.js (red first — write the asserts, run, watch it fail with no module):
    • matchByGuid(['https://theneurondaily.com/p/glm-5-2'], 'https://theneurondaily.com/p_glm-5-2') === the manifest GUID (safe-ified key reconciles).
    • exact key matches exact GUID; an unrelated key returns null.
    • safeGuid('a/b c') === 'a_b_c' (rule unchanged).
  3. In magazine-run.js: replace the local safeGuid body with const { safeGuid } = require('./lib-guid') (keep the name in scope so all call-sites are untouched).

Inline verification:

  • node scripts/lib-guid.js --selftest → prints lib-guid.js --selftest: PASS, exit 0.
  • node scripts/magazine-run.js --selftest → still PASS (safeGuid call-sites unaffected).

Done when: both selftests pass; grep -q "module.exports" scripts/lib-guid.js and magazine-run.js requires it.

T2: Phase-4 prompt — verbatim GUID echo + normalized reconciliation

Spec refs: FR-3.2, D4, AC2 (#functional-requirements). Slice shape: config — SKILL.md prompt-contract change, no end-to-end behavior of its own (consumed by the agent layer at run time).

Depends on: T1.

  1. In SKILL.md Phase 4 (#summarize-tag, ~:180-189): instruct each summarizer subagent to echo the source GUID verbatim in an opaque field (e.g. a fenced guid: line copied from the manifest, never re-derived from the URL).
  2. Specify the normalized-key reconciliation fallback: when assembling the items JSON, match each summarizer result back to its manifest item via matchByGuid (T1); an unmatched result is surfaced loudly (listed, not dropped) per Inv-1.

Inline verification:

  • grep -q "echo the source GUID verbatim" SKILL.md (or the chosen exact phrase) and a reference to matchByGuid/normalized reconciliation in Phase 4.
  • No instruction tells the summarizer to re-derive or safe-ify the GUID.

Done when: Phase 4 names both halves (verbatim echo + normalized fallback) and the loud-on-unmatched rule; structure.test.sh marker (T7) greps the phrase.

T3: render-issue.js issue mode persists the per-issue items JSON sidecar

Spec refs: FR-4.1, D5, AC3 (#decisions). Slice shape: vertical.

Depends on: none (parallel with T1).

  1. Extend the CLI dispatch (render-issue.js:412-423): accept an optional 2nd positional for issue mode — issue <data.json> [<out-html>]. When <out-html> is present: write the rendered HTML to that path (instead of stdout) AND write a sidecar.
  2. Add writeSidecar(outHtmlPath, data): derive <date> from the out filename (2026-06-24_issue.html → 2026-06-24; fall back to data.date), write <dir>/<date>_items.json = {date, file: "<date>_issue.html", items: [{guid, title, feed, type, tags, published, link, bullets, degraded, top_pick}]} (the durable projection; deterministic, no agent step). Write whole-file via temp-then-rename.
  3. TDD (red first): add to render-issue.js --selftest a case that renders a 2-item issue (one with bullets) to a temp 2026-06-24_issue.html and asserts the sibling 2026-06-24_items.json exists, parses, and its items[0].bullets round-trips.

Inline verification:

  • node scripts/render-issue.js --selftest → PASS, including the new sidecar assertions.
  • Back-compat: node scripts/render-issue.js issue <data.json> (no out path) still writes HTML to stdout and writes no sidecar.

Done when: sidecar is written deterministically next to the issue HTML with bullets preserved; stdout-only path unchanged.

T4: render-issue.js library assembles from sidecars + loud skip-notice

Spec refs: FR-4.2, D8, AC4 (#decisions). Slice shape: vertical.

Depends on: T3.

  1. Extend library mode: when the arg is a directory, glob <dir>/*_items.json, read each into the {issues:[…]} shape renderLibrary already consumes (date + file from the sidecar). Retain the legacy library <issues.json> file-arg path unchanged (DL-3).
  2. Loud skip-notice: for every <dir>/*_issue.html with no sibling *_items.json, write one stderr line naming it: magazine library: skipped <file> (no items sidecar — rebuild the issue to include it). Never crash, never silently drop (Inv-1, D8). No HTML or pre-data-guid parser is added.
  3. TDD (red first): add a render-issue.js --selftest case — a temp dir with two *_items.json sidecars + one 2026-06-01_issue.html that has no sidecar. Assert: library output lists exactly 2 issues; the stderr notice names 2026-06-01_issue.html; no throw.

Inline verification:

  • node scripts/render-issue.js --selftest → PASS (captures stderr, asserts the notice fires for the sidecar-less issue and the issue count is 2).

Done when: library is assembled purely from sidecars; sidecar-less issues are named in a loud notice and omitted; no legacy parser exists.

T5: Reuse persisted takeaways on render (overlapping-window)

Spec refs: FR-4.3, D6, AC5 (#decisions). Slice shape: vertical.

Depends on: T3.

  1. Add mergeCachedBullets(snapshotItems, sidecarItems) to render-issue.js (or lib-guid.js consumer side): for each snapshot item already at summarized/rendered, populate its bullets/tags from the matching sidecar item (matched via matchByGuid, T1); return { hydrated, needsStageB } where needsStageB is the subset with no cached summary.
  2. TDD (red first): selftest fixture with 3 snapshot items — 2 carry cached bullets in a sidecar (status rendered), 1 is new (no sidecar). Assert: the 2 are hydrated from cache (bullets equal the sidecar's), needsStageB === the 1 new item only.
  3. SKILL.md Phase 5 (#curate-render): document the reuse path — before Stage B, hydrate already-summarized snapshot items from their per-issue sidecars; only needsStageB items go through summarize. A monthly issue overlapping prior weeks re-summarizes nothing already done.

Inline verification:

  • node scripts/render-issue.js --selftest → PASS (the mixed cached/new assertions).
  • grep -q "reuse" SKILL.md in Phase 5 with the hydrate-before-Stage-B instruction.

Done when: cached items are reused verbatim; only no-cache items are flagged for Stage B; Phase 5 documents it.

T6: Feed-failure tracking + suggest-only quarantine

Spec refs: FR-5.1, D3, AC6, Inv-1 (#decisions). Slice shape: vertical.

Depends on: none (parallel with T1/T3).

  1. magazine-state.js: extend the schema to tolerate feedHealth: { <slug>: { consecFails: N } } (absent → treated as 0). Add helpers: recordFeedResult(state, slug, ok) (ok → set 0; fail → ++) and feedsToSuggest(state, threshold) (slugs with consecFails >= threshold).
  2. magazine-run.js: define const FEED_QUARANTINE_THRESHOLD = 3. In the fetchOne path (:94-108), record a fail in the catch and a success otherwise, keyed by the feed slug (reuse the existing slug resolution). After the discover/prep fetch loop, for each slug in feedsToSuggest(st, FEED_QUARANTINE_THRESHOLD) print one compact line: magazine: feed <slug> failed N runs in a row — consider /magazine remove <slug>. Never auto-disable.
  3. TDD (red first): magazine-state.js --selftest — 3 recordFeedResult(st, 'svpg', false) → feedsToSuggest(st, 3) includes svpg; a 4th call with ok=true resets to 0 and feedsToSuggest no longer lists it.

Inline verification:

  • node scripts/magazine-state.js --selftest → PASS (threshold + reset).
  • node scripts/magazine-run.js --selftest → PASS; grep -q "FEED_QUARANTINE_THRESHOLD" scripts/magazine-run.js.

Done when: consecutive per-run failures cross the named threshold and emit one suggestion line; a success resets; nothing is ever auto-disabled.

T7: Wire selftests + docs (structure.test.sh, SKILL.md Phase 5, issue-format.md)

Spec refs: AC7, AC8, Inv-3, Inv-5 (#invariants). Slice shape: config — test/doc wiring, no new runtime behavior.

Depends on: T1, T2, T3, T4, T5, T6.

  1. tests/structure.test.sh: add chk lines — lib-guid --selftest passes + scripts/lib-guid.js present + magazine-run.js requires it; render-issue.js greps for writeSidecar + mergeCachedBullets + the library skip-notice marker; magazine-state.js greps for recordFeedResult/feedsToSuggest; magazine-run.js greps for FEED_QUARANTINE_THRESHOLD; SKILL.md greps for the Phase-4 verbatim-echo phrase + the Phase-5 reuse phrase.
  2. SKILL.md Phase 5 (:198-199): rewrite the two commands — issue <items.json> {docs_path}/magazine/{YYYY-MM-DD}_issue.html (out-path form, writes the sidecar) and library {docs_path}/magazine/ > …/index.html (directory-glob form). Remove the agent-assembled-issues.json instruction.
  3. Confirm reference/issue-format.md is unchanged (D5 adds a sidecar, not an HTML-schema change — Inv-5); if it documents library assembly, add a line noting the sidecar source without altering the card/library row schema.

Inline verification:

  • bash tests/structure.test.sh → exit 0, every new chk PASS, 0 FAIL.
  • git diff --stat reference/issue-format.md shows no card/library-schema change.

Done when: every new behavior has a structure.test.sh assertion; Phase 5 commands match the real CLI; the HTML contract is preserved.

T8 (TN−1): Dogfood — overlapping-window issue + library on a real fixture

Spec refs: D5, D6, D8, D3, Inv-1. Slice shape: vertical (load-bearing dogfood per Tier 2).

Depends on: T4, T5, T6, T7.

Archetype: CLI real-invocation + Use→blind-LLM-judge.

Scenario: build a temp magazine root with two prior "weekly" issues already rendered (each with a *_items.json sidecar carrying real bullets) + one older *_issue.html deliberately missing its sidecar. Assemble a "monthly" snapshot that overlaps both weeks (already-rendered items) plus 2 genuinely-new items, and one feed (svpg) that has failed 3 runs. Run the real render + library commands.

Objective criteria (exact, measurable):

  • The monthly issue's already-rendered items show bullets identical to their sidecars (reuse, D6) — diff = empty; only the 2 new items would route to Stage B (needsStageB.length === 2).
  • library <dir> lists all sidecar-backed issues and emits exactly one loud stderr notice naming the sidecar-less issue (D8); exit non-error.
  • The new issue writes its own <date>_items.json sidecar (D5).
  • The svpg feed at consecFails=3 produces exactly one suggestion line; no feed is auto-disabled (D3).

Subjective rubric (named dimensions; satisfied bar = all ≥ "honest"): trust (no fabricated or silently-dropped item; every reused bullet traces to a sidecar), honesty of notices (skip-notice + quarantine suggestion read as accurate, non-alarming), coherence (the monthly issue reads as one digest, not a re-run). Judge: a fresh blind general-purpose subagent given only the rendered issue HTML, the library HTML, captured stderr, and this rubric — returns SHIP/NO-SHIP per dimension with a one-line reason.

Done when: all objective gates pass AND the judge returns SHIP on every subjective dimension. NO-SHIP → fix + re-run (≤2 iterations), evidence saved under stories/260624-9fw/dogfood/.

T9 (TN): Final verification

Spec refs: all ACs. Slice shape: config — verification rollup.

Depends on: T8.

  1. Run the full gate: bash tests/structure.test.sh → exit 0, 0 FAIL (note the pass count).
  2. Run every script selftest individually (lib-guid, render-issue, magazine-state, magazine-run) → all PASS.
  3. Force an error path: library on a dir with a sidecar-less issue → the loud notice fires, exit non-error (no crash).
  4. Back-compat sweep: issue <data.json> (stdout, no sidecar) and library <issues.json> (file arg) still work; existing watch.test.sh and bundles.test.sh stay green.
  5. Cleanup: remove any temp roots created by the dogfood that aren't intentionally saved under dogfood/.

Done-when walkthrough: a maintainer runs bash tests/structure.test.sh and sees 0 failures; renders a multi-window issue and observes reused bullets (no re-summarize), a sidecar written, a library built from sidecars with a loud notice for any sidecar-less issue, and a single quarantine suggestion for a 3×-failed feed — with the issue/library HTML visually unchanged for unchanged inputs.

Risks

RiskSeverityMitigation
Sidecar date parsed from the out filename is brittle if the issue naming convention drifts.LowDerive from the _issue.html basename with a data.date fallback; selftest both forms (T3).
Feed-health slug keying must match the cursor slug keying, or health orphans on rename.MediumReuse the existing slug resolution (FR-R4 remapCursors/readFeedsTyped); DL-5; selftest keys by slug (T6).
Story edits both SKILL.md and state.json schema alongside xck.MediumThe B→A (9fw→xck) dependency serializes; the build loop merges xck before 9fw's skill-eval (claim-time transitive-closure merge). Plan assumes xck's state.snapshot + window flags present.
Reconciliation could mis-match two genuinely-different GUIDs that normalize to the same safe string.LowmatchByGuid is exact-first, normalized-fallback, and returns null on no-match (surfaced loudly, never silent) — collision would require two manifest GUIDs differing only in safe-ified chars, which the ledger's GUID uniqueness already precludes.

Review Log

Detailed loop findings: 03_plan_review.md (sidecar). Summary index below.

LoopTypeFindingsDisposition
1Self-review (structural + design)See sidecarApplied inline

Task status is not held here — it lives only in tasks.yaml (D4), which /execute consumes as its work queue.

pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-06-24_magazine-pipeline-robustness/stories/260624-9fw/03_plan.html · Created using pmos‑learnkit
